query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: b30515d8).

## Issue to Resolve

Starting a line with || causes internal error from ktlint --format
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already been reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Expected Behavior
Lines that start with `||` should not crash `ktlint --format --log-level=debug`

## Observed Behavior
An internal error is reported:



## Steps to Reproduce
Proccess this code with `ktlint --format`:

```
package com.my.lint.rules

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode

class BreakKtlintFormatRule : Rule(
    RuleId("my-ruleset:my-rule-id"),
    Rule.About(maintainer = "Me", repositoryUrl = "repositoryUrl", issueTrackerUrl = "issueTrackerUrl")
) {

    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
        node: ASTNode,
        autoCorrect: Boolean,
        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit
    ) {

        if (node.text.contains("this")
            || node.text.contains("that")
        ) {
            emit(node.startOffset, "My violation detail", false)
        }
    }
}
```


## Your Environment
* Version of ktlint used: 0.49.0
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings: None, but am using config generated by `ktlint generateEditorConfig --code-style=android_studio`
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): Command line
* Version of Gradle used (if applicable): N/A
* Operating System and version: macOS 13.2

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem

## Steps to Reproduce
Proccess this code with `ktlint --format`:

```
package com.my.lint.rules

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode

class BreakKtlintFormatRule : Rule(
    RuleId("my-ruleset:my-rule-id"),
    Rule.About(maintainer = "Me", repositoryUrl = "repositoryUrl", issueTrackerUrl = "issueTrackerUrl")
) {

    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
        node: ASTNode,
        autoCorrect: Boolean,
        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit
    ) {

        if (node.text.contains("this")
            || node.text.contains("that")
        ) {
            emit(node.startOffset, "My violation detail", false)
        }
    }
}
```

 88→    /**

 92→    public fun <T> getEditorConfigValueOrNull(

 97→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getDefaultValue() =
 98→        when (codeStyle) {

100→            CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
101→            CodeStyleValue.official -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
102→            CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue

107→     * Checks whether a property with name [propertyName] is defined.

109→    public fun contains(propertyName: String): Boolean = properties.contains(propertyName)

111→    /**
112→     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType]. Returns null if the property type is not found. Can only be used for
113→     * properties for which the name of the property is identical to the name of the type.
114→     */
115→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(): T? =
116→        this.type.getParsedValueOrDefault(this.type.name, this.defaultValue)
117→
118→    /**
119→     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType] and name [propertyName]. Returns null if the property type is not
120→     * found. Intended to be used for properties for which the name is not identical to the name of the property type.
121→     */
122→    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(
123→        propertyName: String,
124→        defaultValue: T,
125→    ): T =
126→        getPropertyValue(propertyName)
127→            .let { propertyValue ->
128→                if (propertyValue.isValid) {
129→                    propertyValue.parsed
130→                } else {
131→                    LOGGER.warn {
132→                        "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' contains an invalid value '${propertyValue.source}'. The default value " +
133→                            "'$defaultValue' is used instead."
134→                    }
135→                    defaultValue
136→                }
137→            }
138→

156→    public fun addPropertiesWithDefaultValueIfMissing(vararg defaultEditorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperty<*>): EditorConfig {
157→        val editorConfigProperties = defaultEditorConfigProperties.toSet()
158→        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
159→        return editorConfigProperties
160→            .defaultProperties()
161→            .plus(

170→     * value of the active code style.
171→     */
172→    public fun filterBy(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>): EditorConfig {
173→        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)

176→            .plus(

178→                properties.filterBy(editorConfigProperties.map { it.name }),

  2→

  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ELSE_KEYWORD
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ELVIS
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.LBRACE
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.LPAR
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.MINUS
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.MUL
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OROR
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PERC
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PLUS
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PREFIX_EXPRESSION
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.SAFE_ACCESS
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.Companion.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 24→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 25→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 26→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfComment
 27→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 28→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline
 29→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeLeaf
 30→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 31→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeLeaf
 32→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 33→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 34→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 35→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 36→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 37→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
 38→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafElement
 39→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 40→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.TokenSet
 41→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.lexer.KtTokens
 42→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 43→
 44→public class ChainWrappingRule :
 45→    StandardRule(
 46→        id = "chain-wrapping",
 47→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 48→            setOf(
 49→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 50→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 51→            ),
 52→    ) {
 53→    private val sameLineTokens = TokenSet.create(MUL, DIV, PERC, ANDAND, OROR)
 54→    private val prefixTokens = TokenSet.create(PLUS, MINUS)
 55→    private val nextLineTokens = TokenSet.create(DOT, SAFE_ACCESS, ELVIS)
 56→    private val noSpaceAroundTokens = TokenSet.create(DOT, SAFE_ACCESS)
 57→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 58→
 59→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 60→        indentConfig =
 61→            IndentConfig(
 62→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 63→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 64→            )
 65→    }
 66→
 67→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 68→        node: ASTNode,
 69→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 70→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 71→    ) {
 72→        /*
 73→           org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement (DOT) | "."
 74→           org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl (WHITE_SPACE) | "\n        "
 75→           org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtCallExpression (CALL_EXPRESSION)
 76→         */
 77→        val elementType = node.elementType
 78→        if (nextLineTokens.contains(elementType)) {
 79→            if (node.isPartOfComment()) {
 80→                return
 81→            }
 82→            val nextLeaf = node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf()
 83→            if (nextLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && !node.isElvisOperatorAndComment()) {
 84→                emit(node.startOffset, "Line must not end with \"${node.text}\"", true)
 85→                if (autoCorrect) {
 86→                    // rewriting
 87→                    // <prevLeaf><node="."><nextLeaf="\n"> to
 88→                    // <prevLeaf><delete space if any><nextLeaf="\n"><node="."><space if needed>
 89→                    // (or)
 90→                    // <prevLeaf><node="."><spaceBeforeComment><comment><nextLeaf="\n"> to
 91→                    // <prevLeaf><delete space if any><spaceBeforeComment><comment><nextLeaf="\n"><node="."><space if needed>
 92→                    if (node.elementType == ELVIS) {
 93→                        node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
 94→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 95→                    } else {
 96→                        node.treeParent.removeChild(node)
 97→                        (nextLeaf as LeafElement).rawInsertAfterMe(node as LeafElement)
 98→                    }
 99→                }
100→            }
101→        } else if (sameLineTokens.contains(elementType) || prefixTokens.contains(elementType)) {
102→            if (node.isPartOfComment()) {
103→                return
104→            }
105→            val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
106→            if (
107→                prevLeaf?.elementType == WHITE_SPACE &&
108→                prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
109→                // fn(*typedArray<...>()) case
110→                (elementType != MUL || !prevLeaf.isPartOfSpread()) &&
111→                // unary +/-
112→                (!prefixTokens.contains(elementType) || !node.isInPrefixPosition())
113→            ) {
114→                emit(node.startOffset, "Line must not begin with \"${node.text}\"", true)
115→                if (autoCorrect) {
116→                    // rewriting
117→                    // <insertionPoint><prevLeaf="\n"><node="&&"><nextLeaf=" "> to
118→                    // <insertionPoint><prevLeaf=" "><node="&&"><nextLeaf="\n"><delete node="&&"><delete nextLeaf=" ">
119→                    // (or)
120→                    // <insertionPoint><spaceBeforeComment><comment><prevLeaf="\n"><node="&&"><nextLeaf=" "> to
121→                    // <insertionPoint><space if needed><node="&&"><spaceBeforeComment><comment><prevLeaf="\n"><delete node="&&"><delete nextLeaf=" ">
122→                    val nextLeaf = node.nextLeaf()
123→                    if (nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace) {
124→                        nextLeaf.node.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf.node)
125→                    }
126→                    val insertionPoint = prevLeaf.prevCodeLeaf() as LeafPsiElement
127→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).treeParent.removeChild(node)
128→                    insertionPoint.rawInsertAfterMe(node)
129→                    (insertionPoint as ASTNode).upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
130→                }
131→            }
132→        }
133→    }
134→
135→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfSpread() =
136→        prevCodeLeaf()?.let { leaf ->
137→            val type = leaf.elementType
138→            type == LPAR ||
139→                type == COMMA ||
140→                type == LBRACE ||
141→                type == ELSE_KEYWORD ||
142→                KtTokens.OPERATIONS.contains(type)
143→        } == true
144→
145→    private fun ASTNode.isInPrefixPosition() = treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == PREFIX_EXPRESSION
146→
147→    private fun ASTNode.isElvisOperatorAndComment(): Boolean {
148→        return elementType == ELVIS &&
149→            leaves().takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }.any()
150→    }
151→}

153→public val CHAIN_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ChainWrappingRule().ruleId

553→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation]s and that those violations can not be automatically
554→     * corrected.
555→     */
556→    public fun hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(vararg expectedLintViolations: LintViolation) {
557→        check(expectedLintViolations.isNotEmpty())
558→
559→        val actualLintViolationFields =
560→            lint()
561→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
562→                .toLintViolationsFields()
563→
564→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
565→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can not be automatically corrected")
566→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedLintViolations.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected = false))
567→    }
568→
569→    private fun Array<out LintViolation>.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean): Array<LintViolationFields> {
570→        return map {
571→            LintViolationFields(
572→                line = it.line,
573→                col = it.col,
574→                detail = it.detail,
575→                canBeAutoCorrected = canBeAutoCorrected,
576→            )
577→        }.toTypedArray()
578→    }
579→
580→    private fun List<LintError>.filterAdditionalRulesOnly() = filter { it.ruleId != ruleId }
581→
582→    private fun List<LintError>.filterCurrentRuleOnly() = filter { it.ruleId == ruleId }
583→
584→    private fun List<LintError>.toLintViolationsFields(): Array<LintViolationFields> =
585→        map {
586→            LintViolationFields(
587→                line = it.line,
588→                col = it.col,
589→                detail = it.detail,
590→                canBeAutoCorrected = it.canBeAutoCorrected,
591→            )
592→        }.toTypedArray()
593→
594→    private fun createKtLintRuleEngine(): KtLintRuleEngine {
595→        val ruleProviders =
596→            setOf(ruleProvider)
597→                // Also run the additional rules as the main rule might have a VisitorModifier which requires one or more of
598→                // the additional rules to be loaded and enabled as well.
599→                .plus(additionalRuleProviders)

Wrote contents to /project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRuleReproTest.kt.

When you're done with your current set of changes to this file, you should call the read_lints tool with the specific file path and fix any newly introduced errors.

content.text:
The issue was caused by a `NullPointerException` in `IndentationRule.isElvisOperator` when an `OPERATION_REFERENCE` node had no children. This situation can occur when `ChainWrappingRule` (which runs before `IndentationRule`) moves a token (like `||`) but leaves its `OPERATION_REFERENCE` parent empty in the AST.

I have implemented a fix by using a safe call `firstChildNode?.elementType` in the `isElvisOperator` function in `IndentationRule.kt`. This ensures that even if an empty `OPERATION_REFERENCE` is encountered, the rule will not crash.

I have verified the fix by creating a reproduction test case that includes both `ChainWrappingRule` and `IndentationRule` using the `android_studio` code style, which was reported in the issue. The test now passes successfully without any internal errors.

### Changes:
- Modified `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`:
    - Updated `isElvisOperator` to use `firstChildNode?.elementType` to avoid `NullPointerException` on empty `OPERATION_REFERENCE` nodes.
- Created `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRuleReproTest.kt` to reproduce and verify the fix.

  8→class ChainWrappingRuleTest {

 10→
 11→    @Test
 12→    fun `Given some method chain which is incorrectly wrapped`() {
 13→        val code =
 14→            """
 15→            val foo = listOf(1, 2, 3).
 16→                filter { it > 2 }!!.
 17→                takeIf { it.count() > 100 }?.
 18→                sum()
 19→            """.trimIndent()
 20→        val formattedCode =
 21→            """
 22→            val foo = listOf(1, 2, 3)
 23→                .filter { it > 2 }!!
 24→                .takeIf { it.count() > 100 }
 25→                ?.sum()
 26→            """.trimIndent()
 27→        chainWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
 28→            .hasLintViolations(
 29→                LintViolation(1, 26, "Line must not end with \".\""),
 30→                LintViolation(2, 24, "Line must not end with \".\""),
 31→                LintViolation(3, 32, "Line must not end with \"?.\""),
 32→            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 33→    }
 34→
 35→    @Test
 36→    fun `Given a newline after an elvis operator`() {
 37→        val code =
 38→            """
 39→            val foobar = foo() ?:
 40→                bar
 41→            """.trimIndent()
 42→        val formattedCode =
 43→            """
 44→            val foobar = foo()
 45→                ?: bar
 46→            """.trimIndent()
 47→        chainWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
 48→            .hasLintViolation(1, 20, "Line must not end with \"?:\"")

 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig

 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY

 25→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent

 36→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 37→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
 38→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafElement
 39→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 40→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.TokenSet
 41→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.lexer.KtTokens
 42→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 43→
 44→public class ChainWrappingRule :
 45→    StandardRule(
 46→        id = "chain-wrapping",
 47→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 48→            setOf(
 49→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 50→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 51→            ),
 52→    ) {
 53→    private val sameLineTokens = TokenSet.create(MUL, DIV, PERC, ANDAND, OROR)
 54→    private val prefixTokens = TokenSet.create(PLUS, MINUS)
 55→    private val nextLineTokens = TokenSet.create(DOT, SAFE_ACCESS, ELVIS)
 56→    private val noSpaceAroundTokens = TokenSet.create(DOT, SAFE_ACCESS)
 57→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 58→
 59→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 60→        indentConfig =
 61→            IndentConfig(
 62→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 63→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 64→            )
 65→    }
 66→
 67→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 68→        node: ASTNode,
 69→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 70→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 71→    ) {
 72→        /*
 73→           org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement (DOT) | "."
 74→           org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl (WHITE_SPACE) | "\n        "
 75→           org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtCallExpression (CALL_EXPRESSION)
 76→         */
 77→        val elementType = node.elementType
 78→        if (nextLineTokens.contains(elementType)) {
 79→            if (node.isPartOfComment()) {
 80→                return
 81→            }
 82→            val nextLeaf = node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf()
 83→            if (nextLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && !node.isElvisOperatorAndComment()) {
 84→                emit(node.startOffset, "Line must not end with \"${node.text}\"", true)
 85→                if (autoCorrect) {
 86→                    // rewriting
 87→                    // <prevLeaf><node="."><nextLeaf="\n"> to
 88→                    // <prevLeaf><delete space if any><nextLeaf="\n"><node="."><space if needed>
 89→                    // (or)
 90→                    // <prevLeaf><node="."><spaceBeforeComment><comment><nextLeaf="\n"> to
 91→                    // <prevLeaf><delete space if any><spaceBeforeComment><comment><nextLeaf="\n"><node="."><space if needed>
 92→                    if (node.elementType == ELVIS) {
 93→                        node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
 94→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 95→                    } else {
 96→                        node.treeParent.removeChild(node)
 97→                        (nextLeaf as LeafElement).rawInsertAfterMe(node as LeafElement)
 98→                    }
 99→                }
100→            }
101→        } else if (sameLineTokens.contains(elementType) || prefixTokens.contains(elementType)) {
102→            if (node.isPartOfComment()) {
103→                return
104→            }
105→            val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
106→            if (
107→                prevLeaf?.elementType == WHITE_SPACE &&
108→                prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
109→                // fn(*typedArray<...>()) case
110→                (elementType != MUL || !prevLeaf.isPartOfSpread()) &&
111→                // unary +/-
112→                (!prefixTokens.contains(elementType) || !node.isInPrefixPosition())
113→            ) {
114→                emit(node.startOffset, "Line must not begin with \"${node.text}\"", true)
115→                if (autoCorrect) {
116→                    // rewriting
117→                    // <insertionPoint><prevLeaf="\n"><node="&&"><nextLeaf=" "> to
118→                    // <insertionPoint><prevLeaf=" "><node="&&"><nextLeaf="\n"><delete node="&&"><delete nextLeaf=" ">
119→                    // (or)
120→                    // <insertionPoint><spaceBeforeComment><comment><prevLeaf="\n"><node="&&"><nextLeaf=" "> to
121→                    // <insertionPoint><space if needed><node="&&"><spaceBeforeComment><comment><prevLeaf="\n"><delete node="&&"><delete nextLeaf=" ">
122→                    val nextLeaf = node.nextLeaf()
123→                    if (nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace) {
124→                        nextLeaf.node.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf.node)
125→                    }
126→                    val insertionPoint = prevLeaf.prevCodeLeaf() as LeafPsiElement
127→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).treeParent.removeChild(node)
128→                    insertionPoint.rawInsertAfterMe(node)
129→                    (insertionPoint as ASTNode).upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
130→                }
131→            }
132→        }
133→    }
134→
135→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfSpread() =
136→        prevCodeLeaf()?.let { leaf ->
137→            val type = leaf.elementType
138→            type == LPAR ||
139→                type == COMMA ||
140→                type == LBRACE ||
141→                type == ELSE_KEYWORD ||
142→                KtTokens.OPERATIONS.contains(type)
143→        } == true
144→
145→    private fun ASTNode.isInPrefixPosition() = treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == PREFIX_EXPRESSION
146→
147→    private fun ASTNode.isElvisOperatorAndComment(): Boolean {
148→        return elementType == ELVIS &&
149→            leaves().takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }.any()
150→    }
151→}
152→
153→public val CHAIN_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ChainWrappingRule().ruleId

102→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] "max_line_length" property based on the EOL Marker which is places at the first
103→     * line of the code sample. If the property has been set before via [withEditorConfigOverride] then that value is
104→     * silently overwritten.
105→     *
106→     * Example of usage:
107→     * ```
108→     *  val code =
109→     *      """
110→     *      // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR

112→     *      """.trimIndent()

116→     * ```
117→     */
118→    @Throws(MissingEolMarker::class)
119→    public fun setMaxLineLength(): KtLintAssertThat {
120→        code
121→            .split("\n")

123→            ?.indexOf(EOL_CHAR)
124→            ?.let { index -> editorConfigProperties.add(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to (index + 1).toString()) }
125→            ?: throw MissingEolMarker()
126→

128→    }
